transcript.text: One of the most important things to consider when you're making short videos and longer videos are in two completely different mindsets. The shorts feed is inherently passive. Viewers are just kind of scrolling. The long-form mindset is totally different. A viewer has to see a video and then decide for themselves whether or not they're going to engage with it. If they click, they're willing to give you 8 minutes, 12 minutes, 20 minutes, and that is just a vastly different behavior from a shorts viewer. And frustratingly, a shorts viewer is completely capable of also being a long-form viewer, able to sit down and give you 20 minutes of their time. And here's what this actually means for you as a creator. For one, you can't just chop a long-form into a short and expect it to work. And you also can't take a short and simply expand on it to make it a long-form video. Shorts are simply a snack, whereas the long-form videos are the meal. Both are great and they can even be cohesive with each other, but people eat one or the other because they're simply hungry for different things at different times.
